title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: They will announce the results at the end of the day.
questions.explanation: Tests the use of the passive voice in the future.
questions.secondSentence: The results (1) _________ at the end of the day.

questions.firstSentence: ‘I won't be late,’ he said to his mother.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs with that-clauses or infinitives.

questions.firstSentence: I only passed the test because I studied all night.
questions.explanation: Tests conditional meaning with 'unless' in a past hypothetical context.
questions.secondSentence: I wouldn't have passed the test (1) _________ all night.

questions.firstSentence: I am certain he was happy with his new job.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for positive deduction.

questions.firstSentence: He had never before seen such a beautiful sunrise.
questions.secondSentence: Never before (1) _________ such a beautiful sunrise.

questions.firstSentence: It's a waste of time to try to open that door.
questions.explanation: Tests the fixed expression 'there is no point (in) -ing'.
questions.secondSentence: There is (1) _________ to open that door.
